ucs.title: [LLM] Lazarus DLL side-loading via trojanized signed app (3CX / X_TRADER supply chain pattern)
ucs.description: Hunts for ffmpeg.dll / d3dcompiler_47.dll / vcruntime DLL loads from a non-Microsoft directory where the loader EXE sits in the same folder — the precise tradecraft Mandiant attributed to DPRK/Lazarus (Diamond Sleet, Labyrinth Chollima) in the 3CXDesktopApp and X_TRADER supply-chain compromises, where a signed third-party binary side-loads a tampered DLL containing appended encrypted shellcode.
ucs.splunk:
`sysmon` EventCode=7 (ImageLoaded="*\\ffmpeg.dll" OR ImageLoaded="*\\FFMPEG.DLL" OR ImageLoaded="*\\d3dcompiler_47.dll" OR ImageLoaded="*\\D3DCOMPILER_47.DLL" OR ImageLoaded="*\\msvcr100.dll" OR ImageLoaded="*\\wlbsctrl.dll") NOT (Image="C:\\Windows\\*" OR Image="C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft*" OR Image="C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Microsoft*")
| eval ParentDir=lower(replace(Image,"\\\\[^\\\\]+$",""))
| eval DllDir=lower(replace(ImageLoaded,"\\\\[^\\\\]+$",""))
| where ParentDir==DllDir
| stats min(_time) AS firstTime max(_time) AS lastTime count values(Hashes) AS DllHashes values(User) AS user dc(host) AS host_count by host Image ImageLoaded
| where host_count <= 5
| convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
ucs.kql:
// Lazarus-style DLL side-loading of ffmpeg.dll / d3dcompiler_47.dll into a non-Microsoft signed app
let LazarusSideloadDlls = dynamic(["ffmpeg.dll","d3dcompiler_47.dll","msvcr100.dll","wlbsctrl.dll","tsmsisrv.dll"]);
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where FileName has_any (LazarusSideloadDlls)
// strip OS / Microsoft legit-load paths
| where FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Windows\"
     and FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Program Files\Microsoft"
     and FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft"
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath !startswith @"C:\Windows\"
// classic side-load: DLL sits in the SAME folder as the loader EXE
| where tolower(FolderPath) == tolower(InitiatingProcessFolderPath)
// 30-day prevalence — Lazarus-tier rarity, not enterprise rollouts
| summarize FirstSeen = min(Timestamp), LastSeen = max(Timestamp),
            HostsLoaded = dcount(DeviceId),
            Hosts = make_set(DeviceName, 25),
            LoaderCmds = make_set(InitiatingProcessCommandLine, 10),
            DllSHA256 = make_set(SHA256, 10),
            LoaderSHA256 = make_set(InitiatingProcessSHA256, 10)
          by InitiatingProcessFileName, FileName
| where HostsLoaded <= 5    // adjust per org size; legit ffmpeg bundles will be wider
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Sentinel ASIM equivalent — image-load events via ImFileEvent are not standard,
// fall back to Sysmon Event ID 7 over WindowsEvent or raw Sysmon channel
let LazarusSideloadDlls = dynamic(["ffmpeg.dll","d3dcompiler_47.dll","msvcr100.dll","wlbsctrl.dll","tsmsisrv.dll"]);
WindowsEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon" and EventID == 7
| extend Image        = tostring(EventData.Image),
         ImageLoaded  = tostring(EventData.ImageLoaded),
         Signed       = tostring(EventData.Signed),
         Signature    = tostring(EventData.Signature),
         Hashes       = tostring(EventData.Hashes)
| where tolower(ImageLoaded) has_any (LazarusSideloadDlls)
| where not(ImageLoaded startswith_cs @"C:\Windows\")
   and not(ImageLoaded startswith_cs @"C:\Program Files\Microsoft")
   and not(ImageLoaded startswith_cs @"C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft")
| extend LoaderDir = tolower(strcat_array(array_slice(split(Image, @"\"), 0, -2), @"\")),
         DllDir    = tolower(strcat_array(array_slice(split(ImageLoaded, @"\"), 0, -2), @"\"))
| where LoaderDir == DllDir
| summarize FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated), HostsLoaded = dcount(Computer),
            Hosts = make_set(Computer, 25), Loaders = make_set(Image, 25),
            Dlls = make_set(ImageLoaded, 25), AllHashes = make_set(Hashes, 25)
          by Image, ImageLoaded
| where HostsLoaded <= 5
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Lazarus-style DLL Side-Loading of ffmpeg / d3dcompiler from Non-System Path
id: f3c3a1d8-3b76-4f4f-9c2f-7c0c1d2e6a01
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects image loads of ffmpeg.dll, d3dcompiler_47.dll, msvcr100.dll or related
  side-load targets from outside C:\Windows and C:\Program Files\Microsoft — the
  side-loading pattern used by Lazarus / Diamond Sleet in the 3CXDesktopApp and
  Trading Technologies X_TRADER supply-chain compromises (G0032).
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0032/
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/3cx-software-supply-chain
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2023/04/20/dev-0196-trading-software
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/13
tags:
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1574.002
  - attack.t1195.002
  - attack.g0032
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: image_load
detection:
  selection_dll:
    ImageLoaded|endswith:
      - '\ffmpeg.dll'
      - '\d3dcompiler_47.dll'
      - '\msvcr100.dll'
      - '\wlbsctrl.dll'
      - '\tsmsisrv.dll'
  filter_system:
    ImageLoaded|startswith:
      - 'C:\Windows\'
      - 'C:\Program Files\Microsoft'
      - 'C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft'
  condition: selection_dll and not filter_system
falsepositives:
  - Bundled FFmpeg in legitimate signed third-party software (Discord, OBS, etc.) — tune by hash / signer / installer path
level: high
ucs.rationale: ffmpeg.dll and d3dcompiler_47.dll loaded from a non-Microsoft folder co-located with the loader EXE is a near-pathognomonic Lazarus IOC: the 3CX (DEV-0139 / Diamond Sleet) trojanized ffmpeg.dll side-load and the X_TRADER d3dcompiler_47.dll with appended encrypted shellcode both fit this exact shape. Generic DLL side-load hunts produce thousands of FPs; constraining the DLL filename, the loader-and

ucs.title: [LLM] Lazarus 'Operation Dream Job' ISO/IMG container → LNK → LOLBin execution chain
ucs.description: Hunts for the Operation Dream Job / Operation In(ter)ception lure chain: an ISO/IMG/VHD container mounted by a user (typically from a LinkedIn-delivered ZIP), an LNK inside spawning a script-host LOLBin (wscript/cscript/mshta/cmd/powershell/rundll32) with encoded or hidden args, followed shortly by external network egress — the recruitment-lure tradecraft Mandiant, ESET and MSTIC attribute to Lazarus (G0032) targeting defense, aerospace and crypto industries.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) AS firstTime max(_time) AS lastTime
    FROM datamodel=Endpoint.Processes
  WHERE Processes.parent_process_name="explorer.exe"
    AND Processes.process_name IN ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe")
    AND (Processes.process="*.lnk*" OR Processes.process="*.iso*" OR Processes.process="*.vbs*" OR Processes.process="*.js*" OR Processes.process="*.hta*"
         OR Processes.process="*-EncodedCommand*" OR Processes.process="* -enc *" OR Processes.process="*FromBase64String*"
         OR Processes.process="*WindowStyle?Hidden*" OR Processes.process="*-w?hidden*"
         OR Processes.process="*DownloadString*" OR Processes.process="*IEX*")
    BY _time host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process Processes.process_path
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| join type=inner host [
    | tstats summariesonly=t count AS IsoCreates min(_time) AS IsoTime
        FROM datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem
      WHERE (Filesystem.file_name="*.iso" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.img" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.vhd" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.lnk")
        BY host Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path
    | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`
    | eval earliest_ok=IsoTime - 1800, latest_ok=IsoTime + 1800
  ]
| where _time >= earliest_ok AND _time <= latest_ok
| stats min(firstTime) AS firstTime max(lastTime) AS lastTime values(file_name) AS lure_file values(file_path) AS lure_path values(process) AS cmd by host user process_name parent_process
| convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
// Operation Dream Job: ISO/IMG/LNK lure → LOLBin chain on the same host within 30 min
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let WindowSec = 1800;     // 30 min — analyst opens lure → executes
let Lures = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where FileName endswith ".iso" or FileName endswith ".img"
         or FileName endswith ".vhd" or FileName endswith ".vhdx"
         or FileName endswith ".lnk"
    // Lazarus drops via browser, mail client or chat — filter the obvious
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe",
                                          "outlook.exe","teams.exe","slack.exe","WhatsApp.exe","explorer.exe")
    | project LureTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, LureFile = FileName,
              LurePath = FolderPath, LureDropper = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              LureUser = InitiatingProcessAccountName;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
// LNK parented under explorer.exe or rundll32 (mounted-ISO browse)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("explorer.exe","rundll32.exe")
   or InitiatingProcessCommandLine has ".lnk"
| where FileName in~ ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe",
                      "cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","curl.exe","certutil.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (".vbs",".js",".hta",".lnk",".iso")
     or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(-w(in)?(dow)?style?\s+h(idden)?|-e(c|nc(odedcommand)?)?\s+[A-Za-z0-9+/=]{20,}|frombase64string|downloadstring|invoke-expression|\biex\b)"
| join kind=inner Lures on DeviceId
| where Timestamp between (LureTime .. LureTime + WindowSec * 1s)
| extend DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, LureTime)
| project LureTime, ExecTime = Timestamp, DelaySec, DeviceName, AccountName,
          LureFile, LurePath, LureDropper, FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, SHA256
| order by ExecTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Sentinel via ASIM — ImFileEvent (lure drop) + ImProcessCreate (LOLBin) correlated within 30 min
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let WindowSec = 1800;
let Lures = ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventType == "FileCreated"
    | where TargetFileName endswith ".iso" or TargetFileName endswith ".img"
         or TargetFileName endswith ".vhd" or TargetFileName endswith ".vhdx"
         or TargetFileName endswith ".lnk"
    | where ActingProcessName has_any ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe",
                                       "outlook.exe","Teams.exe","slack.exe","WhatsApp.exe","explorer.exe")
    | project LureTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname,
              LureFile = TargetFileName, LurePath = TargetFilePath,
              LureDropper = ActingProcessName, LureUser = ActorUsername;
ImProcessCreate
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
| where ParentProcessName has_any ("explorer.exe","rundll32.exe")
     or ParentProcessCommandLine has ".lnk"
| where TargetProcessName has_any ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe",
                                    "cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","curl.exe","certutil.exe")
| where TargetProcessCommandLine has_any (".vbs",".js",".hta",".lnk",".iso")
     or TargetProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(-w(in)?(dow)?style?\s+h(idden)?|-e(c|nc(odedcommand)?)?\s+[A-Za-z0-9+/=]{20,}|frombase64string|downloadstring|invoke-expression|\biex\b)"
| join kind=inner Lures on DvcHostname
| where TimeGenerated between (LureTime .. LureTime + WindowSec * 1s)
| extend DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', TimeGenerated, LureTime)
| project LureTime, ExecTime = TimeGenerated, DelaySec, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          LureFile, LurePath, LureDropper, TargetProcessName, TargetProcessCommandLine,
          ParentProcessName, ParentProcessCommandLine, SHA256
| order by ExecTime desc
ucs.rationale: Lazarus's Operation Dream Job / Operation In(ter)ception (documented by ESET, Mandiant, MSTIC and DOJ indictments) consistently delivers payloads as ISO/IMG/VHD containers carrying an LNK and a decoy PDF — once the recruit mounts the container, the LNK invokes a script-host LOLBin with encoded/hidden parameters. This UC chains the file-drop pattern with the LOLBin spawn under explorer/rundll32 wit
